programmes.show_description: The untold story of session and touring musicians hired by artists like Metallica, Kiss and Billy Joel. Features interviews with Alice Cooper, Rob Zombie, Steve Vai and more.
Simple Minds: At Paisley Abbey, the rock band perform their breakthrough 1982 album, New Gold Dream (81-82-83-84), including the hit Promised You a Miracle. (S1, ep 4)
Former band members of The Doors discuss their self-titled debut album, which featured The End and Light My Fire, and the lyrical genius of Jim Morrison.

programmes.show_name: LSO: Rattle Conducts Brahms And Shostakovich
programmes.show_description: Mechanical marches, pounding brass. An uneasy second movement and a nightmarish vision of life in Stalin's Russia explodes in a frenzied succession of dance themes.
In this art competition, amateur watercolour painters compete against the clock and each other to capture a scene in just four hours. (S2, ep 25)

Parson's Pleasure: Cyril Boggis makes a profitable living as a rogue antiques dealer. But when he buys Farmer Rummins' valuable furniture, his luck may be about to run out. (S3, ep 6)
The Stinker: When millionaire Jack runs into Harold, a boy he bullied at school, he gives him a job. But Harold soon suspects his wife of having an affair with his new boss. (S3, ep 7)
The Rose Garden: A book publisher visits two elderly sisters at their Louisiana mansion. One of them has written a murder mystery that may contain more fact than fiction. (S2, ep 12)
Mr Blanchard's Secret: A mystery writer's imagination gets her in trouble when she turns her overstimulated mind to her new next door neighbours. (S2, ep 13)
A look at the life and career of the Academy Award-winning director, whose work includes Who's Afraid of Virginia Woolf and The Graduate. (S6, ep 2)
A look at the British Academy Award-winning actress who has starred in a host of films including Julia, Mission: Impossible and Murder on the Orient Express.  (S11, ep 3)
I'll Be Seeing You: When Roland leaves his cruel, millionairess wife Vivien for a fresh start with his mistress Anna, the snubbed spouse plans to make his life a living hell. (S3, ep 8)
The Party: When disgruntled factory manager Harry is left feeling undervalued by his colleagues, he decides to take revenge. (S3, ep 9)

programmes.show_name: Wonderland: From JM Barrie To JRR Tolkien
programmes.show_description: In a four-part series, immerse yourself in the worlds of some of history's finest authors, revealing much about their character and the time they lived in. (2 of 4)

programmes.show_name: Stories From The National Portrait Gallery
programmes.show_description: Kate Bryan's exploration takes her to the 18th and 19th centuries, and she discovers how one invention - the camera - changed portraiture forever. (S1, ep 2)
A deep dive into the life and career of the Academy Award-winning director, whose work includes A Beautiful Mind, Frost/Nixon and Apollo 13. (S6, ep 3)
A glimpse into the life and career of the Academy Award-winning actor who starred in All The President's Men, Julia and Melvin and Howard. (S11, ep 4)
Would You Believe It: Two archaeologists working in Jordan get more than they bargain for when they decide to smuggle the statue of a mysterious woman over the border to Israel. (S4, ep 1)
Vicious Circle: When an elderly lady catches a burglar trying to rob her, she decides to help him go straight. But she has to take a different approach when he refuses to change. (S4, ep 2)
John Brown's Body: An upstart furniture designer colludes with his boss's wife to drive her husband mad. (S2, ep 14)
Crackpot: A Good Samaritan helps a newlywed couple with a flat tire. But when the groom accidentally dirties the man's suit, the stranger reveals his true self. (S2, ep 15)
The violin sensation's stunning gala concert from the Kerkrade Stadium, featuring Somewhere Over The Rainbow, O Fortuna and Volare, as well as equestrian riders and fireworks.

programmes.show_name: Andre Rieu: Welcome To My World
programmes.show_description: André Rieu: Maastricht by Moonlight: André produces incredible performances of Conquest of Paradise, La Vergine and I Will Survive with a 150-piece male choir. (S5, ep 3)
Join Barry Cryer as he looks back on the life and career of award-winning actor, musician and comedian Steve Martin, star of Father of the Bride and Little Shop of Horrors. (S2, ep 5)
Expert interviews, historical analysis and readings of his letters trace the evolution of Mozart's genius and his battle for financial stability in the pursuit of artistic freedom.

programmes.show_name: New: Spymasters: The Great Spy Writers
programmes.show_description: New. Kipling to Graham Greene: The world of spies and spymasters is explored, featuring authors from Rudyard Kipling to John Buchan and clips from The Ipcress File. (S1, ep 1)
Richard E Grant: Actor Richard E Grant discusses A Pocketful of Happiness, his memoir recording his wife Joan's illness and death, plus his childhood and Withnail and I. (S1, ep 1)

programmes.show_name: I Hate Jane Austen By Giles Coren
programmes.show_description: Jane Austen is one of Britain's finest authors. Right? Not according to Giles Coren, who tries to convince us she was just a 19th century chick lit hack.
...Vanguard: Bristol Street Art at M Shed. Kate Bryan goes behind the scenes at the M Shed museum in Bristol to discover a new exhibition exploring the city's connection to street art.
Cirque Du Soleil presents the story of a young girl's escape into an extravagant and whimsical imaginary world full of acrobats and colourful characters. Contains flashing images.

programmes.show_name: Phil Collins: Going Back To Detroit

programmes.show_description: Frank and Denise meet Ian Hislop to discover how Pope and Swift created satire that still influences writers today, before exploring Pope's grotto. (1 of 3)

programmes.show_name: Harold Lloyd: Hollywood's Timeless Comedy Genius
programmes.show_description: Documentary about the career and life of Harold Lloyd, the silent film era star who appeared in the notable movies Just Nuts, Girl Shy and The Freshman.
